commit.message:
Fix settings lost on machine restart and update disable not persisting

Root cause: App and MainViewModel each created their own SettingsService
instance, so the VM's saves used a stale default AppSettings object that
overwrote fields not covered by SyncViewModelToSettings (like
IsUpdateCheckEnabled, UpdateSnoozedUntil, LastUpdateCheck).

Fixes:
- Share single SettingsService between App and MainViewModel
- Save window position immediately on drag end (not just OnExit)
- Debounced save on MainWindow resize
- Use IsFirstRun flag instead of fragile != 0 check for position restore
- Remove debug MessageBox left in first-run path
- Replace runtime-dependent SystemParameters default for WindowTop with NaN
- Use atomic temp-file write in SaveSettings to prevent corruption

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: queue user input while LLM is generating, merge and submit on TurnComplete

When status.thinking == true and user presses Enter with plain text:
- Input goes into App::queued_inputs Vec instead of submitting immediately
- Status bar shows '📥 N queued' next to the thinking spinner
- A system message '📥 Queued (N pending — will send when LLM finishes)' is shown

On AgentNotification::TurnComplete:
- All queued inputs are joined with double newlines into one merged message
- The merged message is submitted as a single client.submit() call
- queue is cleared; Ctrl+C/Esc abort also clears the queue

Slash commands (starting with /) bypass the queue and are always handled
immediately (including /abort which clears the queue).

fix: security audit — race condition, PID overflow, UTF-8 panic, input validation (#5)

CRITICAL: locks::acquire() now uses BEGIN IMMEDIATE to prevent TOCTOU race
between concurrent file lock acquisitions.

MEDIUM: PID cast overflow in agents.rs and locks.rs — replaced unsafe
`pid as i32` with `i32::try_from(pid)` + bounds check.

MEDIUM: process_scanner::truncate() now uses char_indices() to avoid
panicking on multi-byte UTF-8 boundaries.

MEDIUM: Added input length validation on send, register-agent, and
context-set endpoints (agent name ≤128, content ≤64K, key ≤256).

ADR-002 documents all findings. ADR-008 copied from monorepo.

commit.message:
fix: address Dependabot and CodeQL security alerts (#7)

* fix: address Dependabot and CodeQL security alerts

Dependabot:
- Remove stale package-lock.json (leftover from old Nx/npm setup; was
  causing alerts for axios, lodash, flatted, minimatch which are no
  longer dependencies)
- Move vite and rollup from direct dependencies to package.json
  overrides to pin safe minimum versions (vite >=7.3.2, rollup >=4.59.0)
  following bun update to vite@8.0.8 and rollup@4.60.1

CodeQL (js/polynomial-redos):
- Replace .* with [^\n]* in all regexes executed against full log file
  content to prevent cross-line backtracking
- Replace (.+)$ with ([^\n]+)$ in inline patterns applied to captured
  substrings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve all ReDoS vulnerabilities in parser regexes

- Replace [^\n]* with [^{\n]* before \{-delimited boundaries to prevent
  backtracking through literal anchor characters
- Replace \s+([^\n]+)$ with \s+(\S[^\n]*)$ to eliminate overlap between
  \s (space) and [^\n] (space) in capture groups
- Add ^ line anchor with m flag to \[([\d:]+)\] patterns so the timestamp
  bracket pattern cannot ambiguously re-match [digit] sequences caught by
  the following [^\n]* quantifier

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: eliminate remaining ReDoS ambiguity in parser regexes

- Drop the 'with ' literal before \{name: so [^{\n]* is bounded by the
  unique '{' character with no possible overlap (most important fix)
- Replace /in\s+(\S[^\n]*)$/ with lastIndexOf(' in ') string split to
  avoid regex quantifier overlap entirely
- Simplify parseWorlds saveActivityRegex by removing the unneeded
  timestamp prefix that introduced [^\n]*SaveActivity calling ambiguity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use unambiguous separator to prevent SaveActivity calling ReDoS

The funcname between 'SaveActivity calling' and 'with {name:' is always
a single non-space identifier (e.g. zwift_network::save_activity).
Replace [^{\n]* with [^ {\n]+ so the quantifier is bounded by a space
character that cannot appear in the match — eliminating all overlap with
the literal ' with {' that follows and removing the polynomial ambiguity.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

useState but the value is keyed by moduleId+projectId+key. Previous
project's cached values are preserved for instant restore on switch-back.

Implementation uses nested Map<moduleId, Map<projectId, Map<key, value>>>
to avoid composite-string key pitfalls. Cache entries are cleaned up on
module dispose (both inline and via registry._disposeSuspended()).

* fix: persist DataProtection key ring across Docker rebuilds (#269)

AddDataProtection() was called without PersistKeysToFileSystem(),
so the key ring regenerated on every container rebuild. This caused:
- Session cookies to become undecryptable (logout)
- Encrypted OAuth secrets in system_config to fail Unprotect()

which is volume-mounted as matrix-keys in docker-compose.yml.
Includes a fallback guard for edge cases where UserProfile is empty.

Also recovered the old key (99f8aade) from the stale
the-matrix_matrix-keys volume into the active docker_matrix-keys
volume, so existing encrypted secrets will decrypt again after
container restart.

* fix: pin Docker Compose project name to prevent volume splitting

Docker Compose derives the project name from the directory name by default.
Running `docker compose` from different directories (e.g. `docker/` vs a
deployment dir) created separate named volumes, causing the OAuth config
stored in SQLite to be stranded in one volume while the app ran against a
different empty volume — prompting the setup wizard on every restart.

Changes:
- Add `name: the-matrix` to all three compose files so the project name
  is stable regardless of which directory `docker compose` runs from
- Add automatic volume migration to update.sh that detects orphaned
  volumes from previous project names and copies data + encryption keys
- Override still possible via COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME or `-p` flag

* fix: remove per-tick timeout that killed Discord gateway after 30 seconds

commit.message:
fix: use Swift-compatible Date encoding for GT3 push-to-start

Swift's default Codable Date encoding uses timeIntervalSinceReferenceDate
(seconds since Jan 1, 2001), not ISO 8601 strings. The ISO string caused
decoding to fail silently on the device, so push-to-start notifications
were accepted by APNs but never created a Live Activity.
